mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP and corporate revenue.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment typically precedes or accompanies economic recessions.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is persistently high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which constrains economic activity and corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation and high interest rates are typically bearish for equity markets.
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 4% and FEDFUNDS is above 4%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth (indicated by GDPC1), positive surprises in housing starts (HOUST) signal robust investment and consumer confidence in the housing sector, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 reactions over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts are a leading indicator of economic activity and consumer sentiment.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows strong MoM growth during periods of high GDPC1 growth, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such events over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key measure of the health of the manufacturing sector.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth (e.g., 3-month average growth > 0.2%) but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth, falling below a critical threshold, signals a weakening labor market. This dampens consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a primary driver of consumer confidence and economic stability.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS MoM change falls below 100,000, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such events over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is declining while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also moderating, it indicates a successful monetary easing cycle that reduces the cost of capital and boosts corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change < -0.25% AND CPIAUCSL annual growth < 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary easing with contained inflation typically supports equity markets.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with contained inflation
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.25% over 3 months and CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 3%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low interest rates (low FEDFUNDS), strong retail sales (RSAFS) growth indicates robust consumer spending unhindered by borrowing costs, which is highly supportive of corporate revenues and S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Low interest rates generally encourage consumer borrowing and spending.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows strong MoM growth while FEDFUNDS is below 2%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of such events over any 3-year rolling window.
